codingTopics: Feeds and ranking: heaps and top-K over content items
Search and autocomplete: prefix tries, typeahead
Streaming: rolling engagement metrics over user activity events
Caching: LRU caches for content items
systemDesignTopics: Design a live-streaming class platform with real-time leaderboards
Design content delivery for the bike/tread tablets
Design subscription billing with hardware-financing tier

coding: At Peloton, given a stream of user activity events, return the top-K trending content items in the last hour using a heap. Discuss memory bounds.
Build autocomplete over content items titles using a prefix trie and return the top suggestions for a typed prefix.
Rank a personalized feed of content items for a user from their history. Discuss the scoring approach.
Implement an LRU cache for content items with a size cap. Discuss eviction and complexity.
Given a stream of user activity events, compute per-user daily streaks with correct time-zone handling.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Peloton scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, hardware-software integration.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a live-streaming class platform with real-time leaderboards.
Design content delivery for the bike/tread tablets.
Design subscription billing with hardware-financing tier.
values: Peloton serves a huge consumer audience. Describe a time you prioritized user wellbeing over an engagement metric.
Tell me about a time you owned a consumer-facing reliability problem.
How do you balance business goals against a healthy user experience?
other: How does Peloton compare to Apple Fitness+ or Hydrow?

rawSummary:
Peloton Interview Guide (2026): Connected Fitness Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Peloton
onepeloton.com ↗

Peloton is the leading connected fitness company — bikes, treadmills, app subscriptions. The interview emphasizes hardware-software integration, live streaming, and the subscription mechanics of fitness content.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a live-streaming class platform with real-time leaderboards

- Design content delivery for the bike/tread tablets

- Design subscription billing with hardware-financing tier

- Coding: medium DSA, often with streaming or scheduling framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, hardware-software integration

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $145K–$180K total

- Senior SE: $215K–$285K

- Staff: $310K–$410K

- Principal: $430K–$570K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Python (backend) and Swift / Kotlin (mobile/tablet)

- Understand video streaming (HLS, DASH) and CDN architecture

- Brush up on real-time leaderboard systems

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Peloton remote-friendly?

Hybrid in NYC (HQ), with engineering offices in San Francisco and Toronto. Some engineering roles remote within US.

How does Peloton compare to Apple Fitness+ or Hydrow?

Peloton has the largest brand. Apple Fitness+ is part of the Apple ecosystem. Hydrow is rowing-specific. Peloton pays mid-tier; comp stabilized post-2022 challenges.

What is the engineering culture?

Mature, customer-focused, calmer pace post-COVID-bubble. Strong work-life balance.
